content.full_text:
CITY OF
SEASIDE

440 HARCOURT AVE.                P.O. BOX 810                SEASIDE, CALIFORNIA 93955-0810

TELEPHONE (408) 899-6200                AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER M/F/V/H


OFFICE OF THE CITY COUNCIL

MAYOR
Lancelot C. McClair


COUNCILMEMBERS
Ira J. Lively
Darlene Burkleo
Darryl L. Choates


                                                                April 18, 1991


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95807-0814

Re:     Proper Use of Funds Contained in Account To Cover Expense

Please examine the enclosed material and render a written legal decision as to the question below:

Is it possible to use funds deposited in an account to cover expenses incurred as a council member
to confer with legal counsel?

The conference with my attorney is to ascertain if the contents of the enclosed is defamatory. I feel
it is an expense incurred because I am an elected member of the Council.

Please render a decision in writing to me at the given address as soon as possible.

                                                                Respectfully submitted,

                                                                [signature]
                                                                Ira J. Lively, Councilwoman
                                                                City of Seaside
                                                                1272 Luzern Street
                                                                Seaside, CA 93955


GATEWAY    TO    THE    MONTEREY    PENINSULA

IRA LIVELY, IS A BLACK RACIST.
(1) SHE VOTED FOR THE K-9 (DOG).
(2) SHE VOTED FOR THE CUR FEW.
(3) SHE IS TRYING TO PASS A
VAGRANCY LAW THAT IS KNOWN
TO BE UNCONSTITUTIONAL, AND WILL
ONLY BE USED TO HARM AND HARASS
PEOPLE OF COLOR.
(4) IRA LIVELY HATES BLACK CHILDREN
AND THE OTHER CITIZENS OF
SEASIDE.
(5) WE DON'T NEED COUNCIL MEMBER
WHO HATE OUR CITIZENS.

By
[illegible] Johnson

You TWO GALS HAVE WORKED HARD IN SENSIBLE TO HELP MY RACIST CAUSE. WE NEED SOME MORE GALS LIKE YOU, ALS NEED TO JAIL MORE BLACKS, WE WANT TITE POLICE TO BEAT BLACK MEN AND CHILDREN LIKE THEY DID THE N-WORD IN LOS ANGELES. YOU GALS ARE IN A GOOD POSITION TO HELP. THE PAY IS GOOD.

WE WILL DO WHATEVER IT TAKES, IF YOU PAY GOOD MASTERS

WE DON'T LIKE BLACK MEN AND CHILDREN EITHER MASTER.

MASTER

IRA                                                DEE

KID HENRY 1991

I can see this is a political cartoon, but much of the text is difficult to read clearly due to the image quality and orientation. Let me transcribe what I can make out:

At the top right, I can see:
"BACK COVER"

Near the center-right area:
"RESERVE
FEB 28 1991
CITY MANAGER
SEASIDE, CA"

On the left side:
"[illegible]"

There appear to be several speech bubbles and labels throughout the cartoon, but most are too illegible in this image quality for me to transcribe with confidence. The cartoon appears to be dated around 1991 based on the visible date stamp.

Due to the poor legibility of most text elements in this image, I cannot provide a complete and accurate transcription of all content while following the instruction to transcribe exactly what is visible.

embedding.qa_text: **OFFICE OF THE CITY COUNCIL** **MAYOR** Lancelot C. McClair **COUNCILMEMBERS** Ira J. Lively Darlene Burkleo Darryl L. Choates April 18, 1991 Fair Political Practices Commission 428 "J" Street, Suite 800 Sacramento, CA 95807-0814 Re: Proper Use of Funds Contained in Account To Cover Expense Please examine the enclosed material and render a written legal decision as to the question below: Is it possible to use funds deposited in an account to cover expenses incurred as a council member to confer with legal counsel? The conference with my attorney is to ascertain if the contents of the enclosed is defamatory. I feel it is an expense incurred because I am an elected member of the Council. Please render a decision in writing to me at the given address as soon as possible. Respectfully submitted, [Signature] Ira J. Lively, Councilwoman City of Seaside 1272 Luzern Street Seaside, CA 93955 IRA Lively, is a black racist. (1) She voted for the K-9 (dog) (2) She voted for the curfew. (3) She is trying to pass a vagrancy law that is known to be unconstitutional, and will only be used to harm and harass people of color. (4) IRA Lively hates black children and the other citizens of Seaside. (5) We don't need council member who hate our citizens. By Ulysses Johnson This image is a cartoon titled "Kid Henry" from 2001, signed by Allyson Johnson. The cartoon features three figures: one labeled "MASTER," and two others labeled "IRA" and "DEE." The MASTER is holding a bag of money and speaking to IRA and DEE. The MASTER's dialogue reads: "You two gals have worked hard in seaside to help my racist cause. We need some more gals like you. We need to jail more blacks. We want the police to beat black men and children like they did the 'nigger' in Los Angeles. You gals are in a position to help. The pay is good." IRA and DEE respond with: "We will do whatever it takes, if you pay good master." "We don't like black men and children either, master." The cartoon uses caricatured characters and dialogue that is highly offensive and promotes racism. It is important to note that such content is deeply problematic and should not be shared or promoted. This image is a political cartoon that appears to be commenting on racial and social issues, particularly in the context of Seaside, California. The cartoon includes several figures labeled with names such as "McClair," "FPPC," "Burkleo," and others, who are depicted in various states of distress or conflict.
